calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Approving Compensation To Former Administrator And Her Counsel
Off Calendar Jul-03-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Senick for Beata Roldan (Petitioner).
John Boessenecker for Melba Cawit (Former Administrator).

Mr. Senick stated they are talking about payment of fees, that Pulqueria (Decedent's Mother) passed away, that he will be filing something to that effect and that the Successor Administrator is not requesting any fees. Mr. Boessenecker stated that Melba is requesting $25,000.00 in statutory fees, $10,500.00 in extraordinary fees and attorney's fees for defending her fee request. Mr. Boessenecker also spoke of representation he provided, stated that there was a problem with lack of communication, a problem with the long time girlfriend of the decedent living in the home, that Beata refused to hire a landlord tenant Attorney and that he would like to file papers for additional fees. Mr. Senick stated that if Mr. Boessenecker's request is granted, he would like to request additional time to respond to the fee request, that Melba was not acting after the bond was released and that the property could have been sold with tenants. The Petition for Order Approving Compensation to Former Administrator and Her Counsel, and the Report on Waiver of Account and Petition for Final Distribution are taken under submission. Counsel will submit fee declarations.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-03-2018 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-24-2018 Continued To May-02-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Approving Compensation To Former Administrator And Her Counsel
Off Calendar Jan-24-2018 Continued To May-02-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Senick for Beata Roldan.
John Boessenecker for Melba Cawit.

Mr. Senick stated that notice went out to the four surviving Siblings other than his Client, that the Mother was noticed on the prior notice, that the other Siblings were not noticed prior to this petition and that he has no information to know if they were informally noticed. Mr. Senick also stated that he received an e-mail from one of the Siblings, that he advised him that he could not represent him, that he is not comfortable to disclose what was in the letter, that he did get a check which he held in his Attorney Trust account, that he is waiting for some procedure from the Court, and that it was a cashier's check and the funds already came out of his Client's personal account. In addition, Mr. Senick stated that he thought the best procedure was to put the check in his account, to disclose it and the Court could tell what to do, and that he believes $400,000.00 was put back/is in the Estate account. Mr. Boessenecker stated that the Administrator sold the house, that she took $1,000,000.00, that she opened her own bank accounts and that she went to the Philippines. Mr. Boessenecker also requested that the Court order the Administrator to put the money in a blocked account within two weeks, stated that she is not paying his Client, but she is paying Mr. Senick and suggested not giving the $30,000.00 back to her. Mr. Senick stated that she is back in the Philippines and that the banks refuse to deal with her while she is overseas. The Court stated that it is appropriate to put the funds in a blocked account as well as the $30,000.00. Mr. Boessenecker asked why not order the Administrator to give the money to Mr. Senick and he place it in the Trust account, and asked the Court to hold off on fees until the funds are placed in a blocked account. The Petition for Order Approving Compensation, etc. and Report on Waiver of Account and Petition for Final Distribution, etc. are continued to May 2, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the blocked account and for argument on fees. Mr. Senick is to prepare an order for the blocked account.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition For Order Approving Compensation To Former Administrator And Her Counsel
Off Calendar Dec-11-2017 Continued To Jan-24-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Senick for Beata Roldan (Administrator).
John Boessenecker for Mabel Cawit (Petitioner/Former-Administrator).

Mr. Senick confirms that the property was given to the Petitioner by assignment from the Philippines, stated that notice was not given to the other Siblings, and that he does not know of any auhtority that requires it, but he can provide notice if the Court wants him to. Mr. Boessenecker stated that he filed a supplement on Tuesday and that he would like to submit the fee issue. Mr. Senick stated that his Client's position is that she does not want to pay anything and that he has some comments. The Court stated that it would rather hear the matter. The Report on Waiver of Account, etc. and Petition for Order Approving Compensation, etc. is continued to January 24, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-12-2017 Continued To Aug-14-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Senick for personal representative Beata Roldan

The Court inquired regarding the duration of Ms. Rolldan's stay abroad. Mr. Senick responded that he believes she will be returning to her home in Philadelphia by the end of June. He requested that the matter be continued so that he could communicate with her and get something on file in July. The matter was continued to August 14, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204, either for status or for an order to suspend the representative.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Court Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar May-01-2017 Continued To Jun-12-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Senick for Beata Roldan (Administrator).

Mr. Senick stated that his Client is in the Philippines, that she is there indefinitely taking care of the Mother, that he is unable to obtain records and that he has no information to provide to the Court. Status on close of the Estate is continued to June 12, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of a report on status of administration. Mr. Senick stated that he would not be able to obtain a signature from his Client.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-28-2015 Continued To Aug-25-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Melba Cawit (administrator) in Propria Persona. Beatalily Roldan and Emmanuel Roldan (sister & brother-in-law of decedent) also present.

The Court stated that this is the 3rd amended first and final accounting, that there are procedural issues, that notice is not proper, that this hearing will need to be continued and asked if there were any objections. Ms. Cawit stated that there are issues regarding the increase of bond, that she submitted verification on 7/20/15 and that all parties have been noticed. The Court stated that it was not sufficient time for today's hearing. Ms. Cawit stated that she tried to submit a verification but that it was rejected by the clerk. The Court stated that procedures must be followed, that the bond needs to be increased but needs to be done procedurally correct and that the ex parte petition is not before it today. Mr. Roldan stated that he and Ms. Roldan were here to request power to sell the property, that they are having issues with Ms. Cawit and that they cannot keep traveling here from Pennsylvania. The Court stated that the issue is not before it today, that they would need to petition for what they are seeking, suggested they speak to an attorney to have it done correctly and clarified that Ms. Cawit is not acting as an attorney in this matter that she is a fiduciary only. The accounting petition was continued to August 25, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow for time to properly notice the petition for that date. The Court stated that it would be heard with the petition already on calendar for that date, reminded Ms. Cawit that she is not acting as an attorney in this case and stated its concern regarding the issue of whether it is appropriate to distribute property to someone in the Philippines who is unable to deal with it.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-23-2015 Continued To Jul-28-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Melba Espartero-Cawit in Propria Persona.

Ms. Espartero-Cawit reported that she filed a response to the examiner's notes today, that she has filed a petition to increase bond, that she has contacted a bonding agency and that a bond will be ready as soon as she has the order. The court stated that the matter will need to be continued for the examiner to have time to review the filings and asked if the real property was being sold. Ms. Espartero-Cawit stated that Ms. Favorito is unable to travel to the U.S. from the Philippines and that once distribution is complete she will speak to her to decide what is to be done with the property. The court reminded Ms. Epartero-Cawit that she will no longer be the administrator after final distribution, that she needs to figure out what to do with the property prior to distribution and asked if there has been any action against the occupant. Ms. Espartero-Cawit stated that per San Francisco rules they must pay the moving expenses of the occupant and that they do not have the money at this time. The accounting petition was continued to July 28, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
